FORM 10-K

For the fiscal year ended December 31, 2024

DISTRESSED CORPORATION

ITEM 1. BUSINESS

We are a specialty retailer facing significant headwinds from changing consumer behavior and increased competition from online retailers.

ITEM 1A. RISK FACTORS

Going concern: Our ability to continue as a going concern is uncertain. We have incurred net losses of $45 million in 2024 and $38 million in 2023. As of December 31, 2024, we had cash and equivalents of $8 million, which management believes is insufficient to fund operations beyond the next six months absent additional financing.

ITEM 7. MANAGEMENT'S DISCUSSION AND ANALYSIS

Revenue declined 35% to $180 million in 2024 from $277 million in 2023. The decline reflects store closures and reduced customer traffic.

Goodwill totaling $2.1 billion, representing approximately 68% of total assets of $3.1 billion, is subject to impairment risk given current operating conditions.

ITEM 8. FINANCIAL STATEMENTS

Report of Independent Registered Public Accounting Firm

Going Concern Uncertainty

The accompanying financial statements have been prepared assuming that the Company will continue as a going concern. As discussed in Note 1, the Company has suffered recurring losses from operations and has a net capital deficiency that raise substantial doubt about its ability to continue as a going concern.

Material Weakness in Internal Controls

Management has identified a material weakness in internal control over financial reporting related to the reconciliation of accounts payable. As a result of this material weakness, there is a reasonable possibility that a material misstatement could occur in our annual or interim financial statements.

ITEM 9. CHANGES IN AND DISAGREEMENTS WITH ACCOUNTANTS

On March 15, 2024, we dismissed Ernst and Young LLP as our independent registered public accounting firm and engaged Deloitte Touche Tohmatsu Limited as our new independent registered public accounting firm.
